description: baseline: DEPTH=8, WINDOW=SSSL, default hyperparams
DEPTH=6, WINDOW=L: smaller model, more steps, less VRAM
TOTAL_BATCH_SIZE 2^17: 77 steps vs 28, massive improvement
TOTAL_BATCH_SIZE 2^16: 157 steps, trend continues
TOTAL_BATCH_SIZE 2^15: 312 steps, diminishing returns
DEVICE_BATCH_SIZE=8, 2^14: 601 steps, tiny gain, 2.5GB VRAM
HEAD_DIM=64: 6 heads vs 3, same VRAM, 726 steps
MLP_RATIO=3: marginally better + fewer params + 767 steps
TOTAL_BATCH_SIZE 2^13: too small, gradient noise hurts
MATRIX_LR 0.05: LR too high, diverges
DEPTH=4 ASPECT_RATIO=96: shallower hurts, depth matters
WARMDOWN_RATIO=0.6: longer warmdown hurts, 0.5 is sweet spot
DEPTH=8 ASPECT_RATIO=48: deeper too slow, 526 steps insufficient
FINAL_LR_FRAC=0.1: residual LR hurts, decay to zero is better
WEIGHT_DECAY=0.1: less regularization doesn't help
ns_steps=3: less precise Muon orthogonalization hurts quality
ADAM_BETAS beta1=0.9: marginally worse, within noise
WARMDOWN_RATIO=0.4: slightly shorter cooldown, 0.5 still better
MATRIX_LR=0.03: too low, 0.04 is the sweet spot
GQA n_kv_head=2: SDPA no GQA kernel on RTX3070Ti, 797s eval
WARMUP_RATIO=0.02: warmup hurts, 541 steps (had GPU contention)
MLP_RATIO=2: too narrow, 3x is sweet spot
no VE: value embeddings are crucial for quality
EMBEDDING_LR=1.0: faster chess token embedding, 847 steps
SCALAR_LR=0.3: within noise, 0.5 is better
LOGIT_SOFTCAP=30: less capping hurts, 15 is sweet spot
ASPECT_RATIO=48: model_dim 384->320, 944 steps vs 847
ASPECT_RATIO=40: model_dim 256 too small, capacity hurts
ROPE_BASE=500: worse than 10000, positional encoding hurt
softcap=10: tighter cap hurts, 15 is sweet spot
DEPTH=5 AR=64: same model_dim=320, 1059 steps vs 944
DEPTH=4 AR=80: same model_dim=320, 1228 steps vs 1059
DEPTH=3 AR=96: same model_dim=320, 1506 steps vs 1228
DEPTH=2 AR=160: too shallow, 1765 steps insufficient capacity
DEPTH=3 AR=80: model_dim 256 too narrow, capacity hurts
EMBEDDING_LR=2.0: too high, fewer steps and unstable
HEAD_DIM=32: 10 heads hurt, 64-dim heads better for chess
MLP_RATIO=4: more MLP hurts, fewer steps dominates
WARMDOWN_RATIO=0.45: 0.5 is still sweet spot
SwiGLU: relu^2 is better for chess, gating hurts
no checkpointing: slower on this hardware, fewer steps
VE all layers: extra VE at middle layer wastes capacity
Muon beta2=0.90: faster second-moment hurts, 0.95 better
WEIGHT_DECAY=0.3: more regularization hurts, 0.2 optimal
ns_steps=7: slower steps with no quality gain vs 5
ADAM_BETAS beta2=0.9: faster AdamW variance hurts, 0.95 better
Muon warmup 300->100 steps: faster warmup hurts stability
ns_steps=6: also worse than 5
Muon beta2=0.97: higher than 0.95 hurts, 0.95 is sweet spot
Muon momentum=0.98: higher than 0.95 hurts, 0.95 is sweet spot
x0_lambdas init=0.05: worse than 0.1; sweet spot confirmed at 0.1
Muon WD=0: scheduled WD decay is important for Muon matrix params
softcap=20: worse than 15, sweet spot confirmed at 15
ADAM_BETAS beta1=0.7: lower than 0.8 hurts, 0.8 is sweet spot
ROPE_BASE=100000: worse than 10000, sweet spot confirmed at 10000
Muon warmup 500 steps: slower warmup hurts, 300 is sweet spot
label_smoothing=0.1: corrupts eval BPB metric (smoothed != standard CE)
gradient clipping max_norm=1.0: slower+worse, no clipping is better
AdamW eps=1e-8: much worse, aggressive 1e-10 critical for embedding LRs
VE gate channels=64: slower (1079 steps) and worse than 32
VE layer 0 only: worse (1153 steps) than both layers 0+2
VE layer 2 only: better than layer 0 alone but needs both layers
cosine WD decay: worse than linear, linear decay to 0 confirmed best
value_embeds WD=0.01: VE needs WD=0 for unrestricted growth
SiLU activation: faster than relu^2 (5.58% MFU), slight improvement
SiLU + MLP_RATIO=4: bigger MLP still hurts even with faster SiLU
SiLU + DEPTH=4 AR=80: SiLU speed enables deeper model, new best (1363 steps)
SiLU + DEPTH=5 AR=64: too slow (883 steps), DEPTH=4 is sweet spot
torch.compile: Triton not available on Windows, crashed at startup
is_causal=True for full attention: 8.49% MFU (was 5.7%), 1768 steps, huge win
DEPTH=5 AR=64 + is_causal=True: 1271 steps, still worse than DEPTH=4
DEPTH=4 AR=96 (model_dim=384): 1418 steps, wider model worse than 320
DEPTH=3 AR=96 + is_causal=True: 2100 steps, more steps but quality loses to DEPTH=4
ns_steps=4: fewer Muon iterations, paradoxically slower and worse quality
HEAD_DIM=80 (4 heads): non-power-of-2 dim hurts SDPA kernel, fewer steps
WEIGHT_DECAY=0.15: thermal throttling, marginal vs 0.2 (inconclusive)
SCALAR_LR=0.7: genuinely worse, 1849 steps confirms thermal recovered
EMBEDDING_LR=1.2: faster token embedding, new best (1868 steps, 8.98% MFU)
EMBEDDING_LR=1.4: thermal throttled (1478 steps), inconclusive
EMBEDDING_LR=1.5: worse at 1630 steps, 1.2 is the optimum
UNEMBEDDING_LR=0.006: too high, lm_head overfits, much worse
ADAM_BETAS=(0.85,0.95): slightly worse, beta1=0.8 confirmed optimal
x0_lambdas init=0.15: causes initial instability, worse than 0.1
LOGIT_SOFTCAP=13: slightly worse (1827 steps), softcap=15 confirmed optimal
ADAM_BETAS=(0.8, 0.98): beta2=0.98 slower variance, worse (1759 steps)
EMBEDDING_LR=1.3: worse than 1.2, embedding LR sweet spot confirmed at 1.2
VE_LR=0.5: slower VE updates hurt, VE needs same fast LR as token embeddings
VE_LR=2.0: faster VE updates also hurt, VE optimal at same LR=1.2 as tokens
Muon momentum=0.90: lower than 0.95 also hurts, sweet spot confirmed at 0.95
TOTAL_BATCH_SIZE=2^13: small batch gradient noise dominates even with faster arch (3260 steps)
UNEMBEDDING_LR=0.002: too low, lm_head underfits, 0.004 confirmed optimal
VE gate channels=16: smaller gate worse, 32 is optimal (1685 steps)
cosine LR warmdown: reaches near-zero LR too early, linear warmdown better (1650 steps)
WEIGHT_DECAY=0.25: worse than 0.2, also thermal throttled (1635 steps)
Muon beta2=0.93: worse than 0.95, sweet spot confirmed (1664 steps, thermal throttle)
Muon warmup start=0.90: worse, 0.85->0.95 warmup is optimal (1666 steps, thermal)
SCALAR_LR=0.6: between 0.5=best and 0.7=close; still worse, 0.5 confirmed optimal (1664 steps)
WARMDOWN_RATIO=0.55: worse, 0.5 confirmed (1654 steps, thermal throttle)
FINAL_LR_FRAC=0.02: residual LR hurts convergence, 0.0 confirmed optimal (1671 steps)
resid_lambdas init=0.9: weaker residuals hurt, 1.0 confirmed optimal (1755 steps)
constant WD=0.2 (no decay): worse, linear decay to 0 confirmed optimal (1720 steps)
x0_betas=(0.8,0.95): more reactive worse than 0.96; smooth momentum confirmed (1704 steps)
parallel attn+MLP: much worse (1558 steps, 5.8% MFU), sequential structure important
GeLU activation: slower than SiLU (1431 steps, 6% MFU), SiLU confirmed optimal
delayed WD decay: worse (1485 steps, severe thermal throttle; linear decay confirmed)
MATRIX_LR=0.042: fine-grained test above 0.04, still worse, 0.04 confirmed optimal
resid_lambdas LR x2 (0.005->0.010): more residual adaptation hurts, slow LR confirmed optimal
VE at even layers (0,2): odd layers (1,3) confirmed better for value embedding placement
ADAM_BETAS beta2=0.92: faster variance adapts worse, 0.95 confirmed optimal
Muon warmup start 0.85->0.80: lower initial momentum worse, 0.85 confirmed optimal
Muon warmup 300->200 steps: shorter warmup hurts, 300 steps confirmed optimal
x0 skip at first layer only: all-layers x0 confirmed better, each layer benefits from original input
x0 skip at last layer only: all-layers x0 confirmed optimal, each layer needs direct input access
LayerNorm instead of RMSNorm: slower (~8% MFU) and worse quality, RMSNorm confirmed
x0 at first+last layers only: all 4 layers need x0 skip, confirmed essential at every layer
SCALAR_LR=0.40: lower x0 LR worse, 0.5 confirmed optimal (0.4 below and 0.6+ above both worse)
LOGIT_SOFTCAP=16: slightly above 15 still worse, 15 confirmed optimal (13<15<16<20)
WEIGHT_DECAY=0.22: slightly above 0.20 still worse, 0.20 confirmed optimal
Muon beta2=0.96: slightly above 0.95 worse, 0.95 confirmed optimal for Muon variance
VE gate bias=True: crashes (1D bias caught by Muon param groups), reverted
WARMDOWN_RATIO=0.48: fine-grained below 0.50 still worse, 0.50 is sharp optimum
WARMDOWN_RATIO=0.52: barely worse than 0.50 (delta=0.0008 vs 0.48 delta=0.005); asymmetric
WARMDOWN_RATIO=0.51: worse than 0.50, confirms 0.52 was luck; 0.50 is confirmed optimal
2-step auxiliary loss (weight=0.1): much worse, extra compute reduces steps, mixed objective hurts
value norm (Q,K,V all normalized): worse than Q,K only; V-norm disrupts VE gate dynamics
x0 after block: pre-block x0 injection confirmed better (post-block anchoring worse)
frozen x0_lambdas (LR=0): learned skip adaptation is important, fixed 0.1 is worse
beta1=0.7 for embeddings only: global beta1=0.8 confirmed optimal, targeted change also hurts
Exp 152: Muon for lm_head (replaced AdamW): extremely slow convergence, lm_head needs AdamW not Muon
Exp 153: SwiGLU MLP (gate_proj*up_proj, MLP_RATIO=2 for param parity): worse than SiLU ratio=3
Exp 154: HEAD_DIM=32 (10 heads vs 5, same model_dim=320): slower, worse than HEAD_DIM=64
Exp 155: VE gate channels 32->64 (more features for gating): slightly worse
Exp 156: EMBEDDING_LR=1.15 (between 1.1 and 1.2): worse, 1.2 confirmed optimal
Exp 157: DEPTH=6 AR=48 (same model_dim=320, 6 layers): close to best but slower convergence
Exp 158: DEPTH=5 AR=64 (same model_dim=320): worse than DEPTH=4 and DEPTH=6
Exp 159: ROPE_BASE=3000 (tuned for chess game length): worse than 10000
Exp 160: MLP_RATIO=4 (larger MLP, GPT-2 default): more parameters but worse, ratio=3 optimal
Exp 161: UNEMBEDDING_LR=0.006 (above baseline): fast early convergence but worse final, 0.004 optimal
Exp 162: x0_lambdas init=0.2 (stronger initial skip): worse, init=0.1 confirmed optimal
Exp 163: WINDOW_PATTERN=SSSL (3 short 1024 + 1 long): slower AND worse, L confirmed optimal
Exp 164: AR=96 model_dim=384 (bigger model, 6 heads): higher MFU but fewer steps, worse
Exp 165: AR=96 384d + EMBEDDING_LR=1.32 (muP-compensated LR): still worse, 320d optimal
Exp 166: seed=123 (different init): worse than seed=42, initialization matters
Exp 167: MQA n_kv_head=1 (GQA is slow on this GPU, 2.6x slower, only ~900 steps)
Exp 168: lm_head bias=True (learnable token priors): slightly worse, bias doesn't help
Exp 169: Learnable RMSNorm gamma (norm_scale scalar_lr=0.5): training instability, very bad
Exp 170: TIME_BUDGET=330s (10% more steps): worse, warmdown phase too long, 300s optimal
Exp 171: WEIGHT_DECAY=0.18 (between 0.15 and 0.20): slightly worse, 0.20 confirmed optimal
Exp 172: EMA decay=0.999: random init contaminates EMA (15.6% weight), incompatible with warmdown
Exp 173: MAX_SEQ_LEN=1024 (shorter context): close but 2048 better for full-game patterns
Exp 174: label_smoothing=0.1: terrible, chess moves are deterministic, smoothing destroys learning
